titleStmt.title: National Library of Sweden, A 134a
titleStmt.sponsor: Medieval Latin Manuscripts in the National Library: Cataloguing and Digitization
titleStmt.funder: Riksbankens jubileumsfond – The Swedish Foundation for Humanities and Social Sciences

publicationStmt.publisher: manuscripta.se: A Digital Catalogue of Medieval and Early Modern Manuscripts in Sweden

collation.p:
VisColl collation. : several missing quires at the end, stubs visible. Eriksson (1937)
presents a different analysis of the quire structure. Eriksson's collation is presented in this VisColl collation.

p.note: Top left corner, in ink by a modern hand: A 134 a., written over NKS 1432. Fol., which is written in pencil; centre page, National Library stamp; bottom left coner: National Library book label with current signum; lower part of page, in brown ink by a medieval hand: ombris adde modis modus est x .

Various partially legible phrases by different medieval hands, e.g. top left corner, in ink by a medieval hand: a skull; below: two lines of text beginning with Decembris mensis facti est peragendum sine pactis?.

binding.p: Medieval binding. Brown leather over wooden boards. 5 double raised bands and endbands. Originally, 2 clasps. Corner and centre pieces with bosses. , in ink: A 134a.
binding.condition: Leather worn and colour almost completely worn off on Spine and LCO. Clasps missing. Endband exposed at bottom and missing at top. LCO, wo deep cuts close to the spine.

origin.p:
Sweden Nydala kloster , 16th century, first quarter. Eriksson (1937)
argues a date between 1506–1508.

provenance.p:
The manuscript was given by bishop Jacob Serenius to Jacob Langebek at some point during the second half of the 18th century., see Eriksson (1943)
. The manuscript was then bought from Langebek's estate by the book collector Peter Suhm in . Then, in the manuscript was acquired from him by the Royal Danish Library, Ilsøe (2007)
.
At the library the manuscript was assigned the shelf mark NKS 1432 Fol., which can be faintly seen on .

acquisition.p: Transferred from the Royal Danish Library to the National Library in as a part of the Swedish-Danish archival exchange program (svensk-danska arkivalieutbytet).

msItem.title: Copies of letters from Nydala monastery
msItem.note:
The majority of the letters are edited in DS 1 and a sellection in a modern edition in Gejrot (1994) , which also presents a survey of the copy-book and editions of the different letters (see Gejrot (1994)
).

supportDesc.foliation: : contemporary pagination in the top margin, 1-190.Items numbered with letters and Roman numerals in the outer margin in red ink. Each parish is assigned a letter (a-i), and each letter a Roman numeral.
supportDesc.condition: Parchment worn and stained. More than half of all original leaves missing. The older pagination indicates 95 leaves, currently 55 full leaves and stubs. See Collation for more information. : a 60mm long slash through the middle of the page.

p.note:
4 notes by a medieval hand. Note 2 concerns events after the Stockholm Bloodbath and begins Anno domini 1521 Martirizatus et submersus fuit venerabilis pater abbas Arwidus; note 3 begins: Anno domini 1524 dominica; note 4 begins: Anno 1526. The notes are transcribed in DS 1
.
Top of page, in black ink by an early modern hand: 5 lines of text beginning with liber preciosus monasterii Nydalensis.
Dates from the main text written in Arabic numerals in the margin by an early modern or modern hand.

decoDesc.p: Main text in black ink, capitals touched in red, occasional rubrics in red.

decoNote.p: : plain initials over 2-3 lines in red.
